notes: Suppresses call-type rubric items that are already owned by the universal NREMT base rubric. BGL check, swallow assessment, and oral glucose are intentionally owned by the hypoglycemia call-type rubric after the semantic duplicate migration. ALS-request applicability is resolved from active agency configuration, not scenario overlay content.

operations.reason: Universal NREMT base rubric owns PPE and scene safety via scene_entry evidence path; call-type item is not credited despite scene_entry being completed, creating a false miss alongside the credited base item.
operations.protocol_ref: NREMT EMT Patient Assessment/Management Medical E202 — scene safety and PPE are base rubric items

operations.reason: Universal base rubric owns general impression; call-type item requires ems_performed_exam source which is not stamped in training context, creating a false miss alongside the credited base item.
operations.protocol_ref: NREMT EMT Patient Assessment/Management Medical E202 — general impression is a base rubric item

operations.reason: Universal base rubric owns LOC/AVPU assessment; both base (1pt) and call-type (3pt) items were credited for the same student action, producing double-credit for a single clinical assessment.
operations.protocol_ref: NREMT EMT Patient Assessment/Management Medical E202 — LOC/responsiveness is a base rubric item

operations.reason: Universal base rubric owns primary airway and breathing assessment; both base (3pt) and call-type (2pt) items were credited for the same student action, producing double-credit.
operations.protocol_ref: NREMT EMT Patient Assessment/Management Medical E202 — airway and breathing assessment is a base rubric item

operations.reason: Scenario item peds_diabetic_emergency_01.history_diabetes (3pt) covers Type 1 diabetes and insulin pump history; both scenario and call-type items were credited for the same history questions, producing double-credit. Migrate when scenario item is removed.
operations.protocol_ref: NASEMSO Hypoglycemia Rubric v1 — hpi_diabetic_history superseded by scenario-specific item pending migration
